name: West Meon
overview: West Meon is a vibrant village in the upper Meon Valley within the South Downs National Park, in East Hampshire, roughly 6-7 miles from Petersfield on the A272/A32 crossroads. It takes its name from the River Meon, a chalk stream that winds through the South Downs. Thomas Lord, founder of Lord's Cricket Ground, retired to and is buried in the village, which is well known for its eponymous pub, The Thomas Lord.
character: A delightful, active South Downs village with a strong community, country pubs and a notable amateur dramatic and societies scene, set amid prime walking and shooting country.
amenities: The West Meon (pub on the A272/A32 crossroads)
West Meon Community Shop and cafe (open 7 days)
Numerous sports clubs and an amateur dramatic group
Meon Hut service station/convenience store nearby

schools.name: Secondary schools in Petersfield/Bishop's Waltham area

commutes.notes: ~6-7 miles via A272; Petersfield has a mainline station

prices.summary: A popular South Downs village; period cottages and some fine listed houses (including Grade II* country houses on the fringes) command strong values. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walking and riding in the South Downs National Park
Trout fishing on the River Meon
The Thomas Lord pub and Thomas Lord's grave
Shooting and country pursuits in the area
pros: Two pubs plus a thriving community shop and cafe
Primary school and strong societies/sports scene
Good A272 links to Petersfield and Winchester
cons: No railway station; rely on Petersfield
Secondary schooling is out of the village
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a lively, well-served South Downs village with pubs, a community shop and excellent walking, within reach of Petersfield rail.
primarySupermarket: West Meon Community Shop; supermarkets in Petersfield

sources.label: West Meon Parish Council - Village Shop
